endpoints.name: Get recent media from a custom geo-id.
endpoints.description:
Get recent media from a geography subscription that you created.

**Note:** You can only access Geographies that were explicitly created by your OAuth client. Check the
Geography Subscriptions section of the [real-time updates page](https://instagram.com/developer/realtime/).
When you create a subscription to some geography that you define, you will be returned a unique `geo-id` that
can be used in this query. To backfill photos from the location covered by this geography, use the
[media search endpoint](https://instagram.com/developer/endpoints/media/).

**Warning:** [Deprecated](http://instagram.com/developer/changelog/) for Apps created **on or after** Nov 17, 2015
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{GEO-ID}} with a real geo-id.

endpoints.name: Search for a location by geographic coordinate.
endpoints.description: Search for a location by geographic coordinate.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get information about a location.
endpoints.description: Get information about a location.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{LOCATION-ID}} with a real location-id.

endpoints.name: Get a list of recent media objects from a given location.
endpoints.description: Get a list of recent media objects from a given location.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{LOCATION-ID}} with a real location-id.

endpoints.name: Get a list of currently popular media.
endpoints.description:
Get a list of what media is most popular at the moment. Can return mix of `image` and `video` types.

**Warning:** [Deprecated](http://instagram.com/developer/changelog/) for Apps created **on or after** Nov 17, 2015
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY.

endpoints.name: Search for media in a given area.
endpoints.description:
Search for media in a given area. The default time span is set to 5 days. The time span must not exceed 7 days.
Defaults time stamps cover the last 5 days. Can return mix of `image` and `video` types.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get information about a media object.
endpoints.description:
This endpoint returns the same response as `GET /media/{media-id}`.

A media object's shortcode can be found in its shortlink URL. An example shortlink is
`http://instagram.com/p/D/`, its corresponding shortcode is `D`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{SHORTCODE}} with a real shortcode.

endpoints.name: Get information about a media object.
endpoints.description:
Get information about a media object. The returned type key will allow you to differentiate between image and
video media.

**Note:** if you authenticate with an OAuth Token, you will receive the user_has_liked key which quickly tells
you whether the current user has liked this media item.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{MEDIA-ID}} with a real media-id.

endpoints.name: Get a list of recent comments on a media object.
endpoints.description: Get a list of recent comments on a media object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{MEDIA-ID}} with a real media-id.

endpoints.name: Create a comment on a media object.
endpoints.description:
Create a comment on a media object with the following rules:

  * The total length of the comment cannot exceed 300 characters.
  * The comment cannot contain more than 4 hashtags.
  * The comment cannot contain more than 1 URL.
  * The comment cannot consist of all capital letters.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{MEDIA-ID}} with a real media-id.

endpoints.name: Remove a comment.
endpoints.description: Remove a comment either on the authenticated user's media object or authored by the authenticated user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{MEDIA-ID}} with a real media-id. Replace {{COMMENT-ID}} with a real comment-id.

endpoints.name: Get a list of users who have liked this media.
endpoints.description: Get a list of users who have liked this media.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{MEDIA-ID}} with a real media-id.

endpoints.name: Set a like on this media by the current user.
endpoints.description: Set a like on this media by the currently authenticated user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{MEDIA-ID}} with a real media-id.

endpoints.name: Remove a like on this media by the current user.
endpoints.description: Remove a like on this media by the currently authenticated user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{MEDIA-ID}} with a real media-id.

endpoints.name: Search for tags by name.
endpoints.description: Search for tags by name.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get information about a tag object.
endpoints.description: Get information about a tag object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{TAG-NAME}} with a real tag-name.

endpoints.name: Get a list of recently tagged media.
endpoints.description:
Get a list of recently tagged media. Use the `max_tag_id` and `min_tag_id` parameters in the pagination
response to paginate through these objects.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{TAG-NAME}} with a real tag-name.

endpoints.name: Search for a user by name.
endpoints.description: Search for a user by name.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY.

endpoints.name: See the authenticated user's feed.
endpoints.description:
See the authenticated user's feed.

**Warning:** [Deprecated](http://instagram.com/developer/changelog/) for Apps created **on or after** Nov 17, 2015
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY.

endpoints.name: See the list of media liked by the authenticated user.
endpoints.description:
See the list of media liked by the authenticated user. Private media is returned as long as the authenticated
user has permission to view that media. Liked media lists are only available for the currently authenticated
user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY.

endpoints.name: List the users who have requested this user's permission to follow.
endpoints.description: List the users who have requested this user's permission to follow.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get basic information about a user.
endpoints.description:
Get basic information about a user. To get information about the owner of the access token, you can use
**self** instead of the `user-id`.

Security scope `public_content` is required to read information about other users.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{USER-ID}} with a real user-id.

endpoints.name: Get the list of users this user is followed by.
endpoints.description:
Get the list of users this user is followed by. To get users followed by the owner of the access token, you
can use **self** instead of the `user-id`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{USER-ID}} with a real user-id.

endpoints.name: Get the list of users this user follows.
endpoints.description:
Get the list of users this user follows. To get follows of the owner of the access token, you can use **self**
instead of the `user-id`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{USER-ID}} with a real user-id.

endpoints.name: Get the most recent media published by a user.
endpoints.description:
Get the most recent media published by a user. To get the most recent media published by the owner of the
access token, you can use **self** instead of the `user-id`.

Security scope `public_content` is required to read information about other users.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{USER-ID}} with a real user-id.

endpoints.name: Get information about a relationship to another user.
endpoints.description: Get information about a relationship to another user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{USER-ID}} with a real user-id.

endpoints.name: Modify the relationship between the current user and the target user.
endpoints.description: Modify the relationship between the current user and the target user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INSTAGRAM_API_KEY}} with your INSTAGRAM API KEY. Replace {{USER-ID}} with a real user-id.
